articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Colombia edges Argentina in Copa América final rematch
article.description: James Rodríguez scored from the penalty spot to seal Colombia's 2-1 win over defending World Cup and Copa América champions Argentina on Tuesday in a South American World Cup qualifier.
article.linkText: James, Colombia edge Argentina in Copa rematch

images.name: James celeb Colombia Argentina_240909 [1296x729]
images.caption: James Rodriguez celebrates after scoring a goal for Colombia against Argentina.
images.alt: James Rodriguez celebrates after scoring a goal for Colombia against Argentina.

article.story:
James Rodríguez scored from the penalty spot to seal Colombia's 2-1 win over defending World Cup and Copa América champions Argentina on Tuesday in a South American World Cup qualifier.

Injured Argentina star Lionel Messi didn't play in Barranquilla in what was the first match between the teams since the Copa América final.

Yerson Mosquera opened the scoring in the 25th minute, but Argentina equalized in the 48th when Nico Gonzalez capitalized on a defensive blunder from the Colombians. James' 60th-minute penalty secured the win at the Metropolitano Stadium.

World Cup champion Argentina lifted the Copa América trophy after a 1-0 overtime victory over Colombia in July.

Colombia, which hadn't beaten Argentina since 2019, is still unbeaten in South American World Cup qualifying.

Argentina leads the round-robin competition with 18 points after eight matches, two point clear of Colombia.

The top six teams in South American qualifying will get automatic spots at the 2026 World Cup.

Colombia once again had the 33-year-old Rodríguez to thank after he gave a perfect cross for Mosquera to nod in and opening the scoring. That equalized Carlos Valderrama's record of 11 assists in South American World Cup qualifying.

A mistake by Rodríguez allowed Argentina to equalize, with Nico González stealing the ball from him and shooting past goalkeeper Camilo Vargas.

But Rodríguez made amends when he beat Emiliano Martínez, a prolific penalty stopper, and equalized Falcao García's mark of 13 goals in World Cup qualifying.

Argentina's strikers Lautaro Martínez and Julián Álvarez failed to offer as much danger as they do when veteran Messi is there to create opportunities for them.

Information from The Associated Press was used in this story.

keyEvents.text: Gonzalo Montiel (Argentina) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Colombia 1, Argentina 0. Yerson Mosquera (Colombia) header from very close range to the top right corner. Assisted by James Rodríguez with a cross following a corner.
Substitution, Argentina. Nahuel Molina replaces Gonzalo Montiel.
Goal! Colombia 1, Argentina 1. Nicolás González (Argentina) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner.
Goal! Colombia 2, Argentina 1. James Rodríguez (Colombia) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the top right corner.
Substitution, Argentina. Marcos Acuña replaces Lisandro Martínez.
Substitution, Argentina. Alexis Mac Allister replaces Leandro Paredes.
Substitution, Argentina. Giovani Lo Celso replaces Rodrigo De Paul.
Jhon Durán (Colombia) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Colombia. Jhon Córdoba replaces Jhon Durán.
Substitution, Colombia. Kevin Castaño replaces Jhon Arias.
Substitution, Argentina. Paulo Dybala replaces Enzo Fernández.
Substitution, Colombia. Juan Cabal replaces Yerson Mosquera because of an injury.
Camilo Vargas (Colombia) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Colombia. Juan Portilla replaces James Rodríguez.
Cristian Romero (Argentina) is shown the yellow card.
